project.name: MochaMe-KMP
project.description: A proof-of-concept exploration into Kotlin Multiplatform. The goal is to build an isolated, privacy-centric, local-first sync engine deployable across platforms using minimal platform-specific boilerplate, with the application incorporating swappable edge AI inference.

layers.name: Sync Engine
layers.description: Core local-first synchronization logic and contracts.

layers.name: Features
layers.description: Pure Kotlin feature modules and data schemas.

layers.name: UI
layers.description: Compose Multiplatform UI components and view models.

layers.name: Entry Points
layers.description: Platform-specific application entry points and assembly modules.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Cross-cutting concerns, system orchestrators, and platform abstractions.

layers.name: Build Logic
layers.description: Gradle build scripts, convention plugins, and version catalogs.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here to understand the high-level architecture, goals, and setup of MochaMe-KMP. This document provides the foundational context for the entire repository.

tour.title: Shared UI Entry
tour.description: Explore the shared entry point for the user interface. This is where the Compose Multiplatform App is defined, serving as the visual front for all platforms.

tour.title: Core Sync Engine
tour.description: Dive into the heart of MochaMe: the local-first synchronization engine. This step covers the coordinator that manages sync cycles and the gateways for data transfer.

tour.title: System Orchestration
tour.description: Learn how the system manages node context and boot lifecycle. The orchestrator ensures that all internal components are correctly initialized and contextualized.

tour.title: Feature Implementation (Bio)
tour.description: Examine a concrete feature implementation. The 'Bio' feature demonstrates how domain models, repositories, and data access objects (DAOs) are structured in this codebase.

tour.title: Platform Assembly
tour.description: See how the disparate modules are wired together using Dependency Injection (Koin) and platform-specific bridges to form a functional application.

tour.title: Build Logic
tour.description: Understand the custom Gradle build logic that powers the multi-module KMP setup. These convention plugins ensure consistency across features and assembly modules.
